message:
feat(types,vdbe): precomputed record headers for fast-path INSERT

Replace the old RecordHeaderTemplate (raw byte vector) with a typed
PrecomputedRecordHeader that enables zero-copy header patching during
MakeRecord execution, eliminating per-row header recomputation for
INSERT-heavy workloads with fixed schemas.

PrecomputedRecordHeader (types/record.rs):
- PrecomputedSerialTypeKind enum: NullPlaceholder, IntegerOrNull,
  RealOrNull — each knows its header varint length, max payload
  length, and runtime serial-type-byte mapping
- PrecomputedRecordHeader struct: pre-built header template with
  per-column slot offsets for runtime patching
- serialize_record_iter_with_precomputed_header_into(): fast-path
  serializer that memcpys the template, patches serial types in-place,
  writes payload bytes; returns false on contract mismatch (caller
  falls back to generic path)
- Proptest: 512-case verification that precomputed encoding matches
  the generic serialize_record() output exactly

P4 opcode variant (types/opcode.rs):
- P4::RecordHeaderTemplate(Vec<u8>) → P4::PrecomputedHeader(
  PrecomputedRecordHeader) with updated display formatting

Codegen integration (vdbe/codegen.rs):
- try_build_precomputed_record_header() returns PrecomputedRecordHeader
  instead of raw Vec<u8>
- precomputed_serial_type_kind() supports IntegerOrNull and RealOrNull
  (previously only REAL NOT NULL was handled)
- STRICT TEXT columns correctly fall back to affinity-based path

Engine hot path (vdbe/engine.rs):
- execute_make_record_hot() rewritten as 3-way match: PrecomputedHeader
  tries fast path first, falls back to generic on contract mismatch;
  Affinity and default arms unchanged
- estimate_make_record_buffer_capacity() updated for PrecomputedHeader
- INSERT path: mark_storage_root_page_dirty(root_page) replaces
  sync_storage_table_insert_into_memdb_mirror() for direct dirty
  marking when root page is known
- New FSQLITE_VDBE_INSERT_APPEND_HINT_CLEAR_COUNT counter

Benchmark (vdbe/benches/make_record.rs):
- Criterion benchmark comparing generic vs precomputed serialization
  for 4/8/16/32-column INTEGER schemas

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -58,5 +58,9 @@ harness = false
 name = "vectorized_dispatch"
 harness = false
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "make_record"
+harness = false
+
 [lints]
 workspace = true
